architecture_summary: Point Transformer designs a self-attention layer for 3D point clouds using local kNN neighborhoods, vector attention, and trainable relative position encoding, and builds residual point transformer blocks into encoder-decoder networks for classification and dense prediction.

novel_contributions: We design a highly expressive Point Transformer layer for point cloud processing.
The layer is invariant to permutation and cardinality and is thus inherently suited to point cloud processing.
Based on the Point Transformer layer, we construct high-performing Point Transformer networks for classification and dense prediction on point clouds.
We report extensive experiments over multiple domains and datasets and set the new state of the art on multiple highly competitive benchmarks.

key_claims.claim: Point Transformer applies self-attention to 3D point clouds using local neighborhoods, vector attention, and trainable relative position encoding.
key_claims.evidence_section: 3. Point Transformer / 3.2. Point Transformer Layer / 3.3. Position Encoding

key_claims.claim: The resulting networks are based entirely on point transformer layers, pointwise transformations, and pooling, without convolutions for preprocessing or auxiliary branches.
Point Transformer sets the new state of the art on S3DIS, ModelNet40, and ShapeNetPart.
On S3DIS Area 5, Point Transformer attains 70.4% mIoU and crosses the 70% mIoU threshold for the first time.
On S3DIS 6-fold cross-validation, Point Transformer reaches 73.5% mIoU and outperforms the prior state of the art by 2.9 absolute percentage points.
On ModelNet40, Point Transformer achieves 93.7% overall accuracy and sets the new state of the art in both metrics.
On ShapeNetPart, Point Transformer achieves 86.6 instance mIoU and outperforms all prior models as measured by instance mIoU.
The paper reports that vector attention is significantly better than scalar attention on S3DIS Area 5, with 70.4% vs. 64.6% mIoU.
